title: Validating and Normalizing HTML Anchor Link Attributes

statement.markdown:
Implement a function that validates and normalizes HTML anchor link attributes. The function should take an object of attributes as input, where each key is an attribute name and each value is the corresponding attribute value. The function should return a new object with the validated and normalized attributes.

The function should check for the following:

- The 'href' attribute should be a valid URL. You can use the URL class from the URL API to check for validity.
- The 'rel' attribute should be a string of comma-separated values, where each value is a valid relation type. You can use a list of valid relation types to check for validity.
- The 'target' attribute should be either '_self' or '_blank'.

The function should also normalize the 'href' attribute by removing any unnecessary protocol or host information, and the 'rel' attribute by removing any duplicate or empty values.

For example, if the input attributes are:

```javascript
{ href: 'https://example.com', rel: 'noopener,noreferrer', target: '_blank' }
```

The function should return:

```javascript
{ href: 'example.com', rel: 'noopener,noreferrer', target: '_blank' }
```

Note that the 'href' attribute has been normalized to remove the protocol and host information, and the 'rel' attribute has been normalized to remove any duplicate or empty values.
statement.inputFormat: The function takes an object of attributes as input, where each key is an attribute name and each value is the corresponding attribute value.
statement.outputFormat: The function returns a new object with the validated and normalized attributes.
statement.notes: You can use the URL class from the URL API to check for validity of the 'href' attribute.

constraints.description: Number of attributes in the input object

examples.explanation: The 'href' attribute has been normalized to remove the protocol and host information, and the 'rel' attribute has been normalized to remove any duplicate or empty values.
The 'href' attribute is not a valid URL, so it has not been normalized.
The 'rel' attribute has been normalized to remove the empty value.

hints.text: First hint text: Check the 'href' attribute for validity using the URL class from the URL API.
Second hint text: Use a set to remove any duplicate values from the 'rel' attribute.
